claim_bullets.bullet_text: JavaScript uses the 'call by value' evaluation strategy. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.40-41)
In call by value, JavaScript evaluates expressions before applying functions to the resulting values. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.40-41)
Example: ((diameter) => diameter * 3.14159265)(1 + 1) evaluates 1 + 1 to 2 before applying the function. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.40-41)

source_record_text: Like most contemporary programming languages, JavaScript uses the 'call by value' evaluation strategy. That means that when you write some code that appears to apply a function to an expression or expressions, JavaScript evaluates all of those expressions and applies the functions to the resulting value(s).
